title: Fishmongers and poultry dressers
description: Cut, trim, or prepare consumer-sized portions of meat for use or sale in retail establishments. Use hands or hand tools to perform routine cutting and trimming of meat, poultry, and seafood. Perform nonroutine or precision functions involving the preparation of large portions of meat. Work may include specialized slaughtering tasks, cutting standard or premium cuts of meat for marketing, making sausage, or wrapping meats. Work typically occurs in slaughtering, meat packing, or wholesale establishments. (Based on 4 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Wrap, weigh, label, price cuts of meat, and tag containers for weight and contents.
Prepare ready-to-heat foods by filleting meat or fish or cutting it into bite-sized pieces, and adding vegetables or sauces.
Prepare and place meat cuts and products in display counter to appear attractive and catch the shopper's eye.
Remove bones and cut meat into standard cuts in preparation for marketing.
Cut, trim, skin, sort, and wash viscera of slaughtered animals to separate edible portions from offal.
Eviscerate, trim carcasses of slaughtered animals, and cut, trim, or skin sections of animals.
Tend assembly lines to process carcasses and perform various cuts needed for processing.
Prepare special cuts of meat ordered by customers and grind meat into hamburger, trimmings, or ready-to-cook forms.
Inspect meat products for defects, bruises, or blemishes, remove excess fat, and estimate requirements to order or requisition meat supplies.
Use knives, cleavers, meat saws and other equipment for meat cutting and trimming, and fill or remove product from trays, carts, or equipment.
Signal coworkers for synchronized flow of materials and observe material flow for machine malfunction and safety issues.
Record quantity of meat received and issued to cooks or keep records of meat sales, and maintain production data.
Store and ensure meat quality upon delivery and supervise other butchers or meat cutters as needed.
Clean, trim, slice, and prepare carcasses for further processing, while curing, smoking, or preserving meat.
Remove non-meat portions, such as skin or bones, from carcasses, and wrap dressed carcasses or meat cuts as required.
Perform specialized tasks related to slaughtering, including stunning animals and severing jugular veins, and clean animal hides.
Smooth out products in bins or trays to prepare for the next stage of processing and maintain equipment cleanliness.
